AC-1.description: Sidebar Categorizer group no longer exposes separate Bulk Categorization, Batch Jobs, and Individual Jobs entries — replaced by one Categorization panel

AC-2.description: Unified panel provides run actions for Process Uncategorized, Process All, and Test Webhook without navigating to a different sidebar section

AC-3.description: Single transaction scope control expresses expense/income intent and honors Skip Deposits from General Settings

AC-4.description: Integrated job monitor lists batch jobs and individual jobs together with type badges, live Socket.io updates, batch pause/resume/cancel controls

AC-5.description: Starting a bulk run or webhook test keeps the operator on the unified panel

AC-6.description: Existing REST endpoints remain callable without contract change

AC-7.description: Side-panel nav groups (Categorizer / Special tools / Maintenance) and collapsible-section interaction model preserved

AC-8.notes: User guide creation is execute-phase work, not sprint-plan work. Task will be created during execute phase.

gaps.description: AC-8 (user guide documentation) is not covered by any task in the sprint plan
gaps.resolution: This is expected — user guide creation is execute-phase work. The sprint-plan phase correctly defers this to execute. No action required at plan-verify phase.

DEC-0015.title: Panel Merge Strategy
DEC-0015.notes: Tasks correctly implement unified panel-categorization replacing panel-manual, panel-batch, panel-individual. Sidebar entries updated per decision.

DEC-0016.title: Scope Control Approach
DEC-0016.notes: Tasks correctly implement segmented control (Withdrawals/Deposits/Both) with additive scope field. Backend filter logic honors auto-skip-deposits config.

DEC-0017.title: Test Webhook Integration
DEC-0017.notes: Tasks correctly move Test Webhook form to unified panel, remove test-type select, and inherit scope from panel selector.

dependency_graph.notes: Dependency graph is logical and complete. T-0032 is the foundation task. T-0037/T-0038 depend on T-0032 (DOM structure). T-0039 depends on T-0037/T-0038 (frontend handlers must be ready before backend can accept scope field).

risks.description: Monolithic HTML refactor (~5.9k LOC, no UI tests)
risks.mitigation: Manual regression checklist in tasks.md; test batch controls, webhook, scope selector
Preserve render functions unchanged; only move DOM containers

risks.description: Socket.io routing break if DOM IDs change
risks.mitigation: Keep mount and batch-mount IDs exact

risks.description: Scope field ignored by existing API callers
risks.mitigation: scope is additive/optional; absent → existing behavior

risks.description: test-type removal breaks bookmarked UI state
risks.mitigation: Document in user guide; scope replaces type selection

risks.description: Auto-jump removal confuses users expecting panel switch
risks.mitigation: Toast notification replaces auto-jump; progress visible inline

findings.description: AC-8 (user guide) is not covered by any task in the sprint plan
findings.impact: No impact on sprint execution — user guide creation is execute-phase work
findings.recommendation: During execute phase, create a task to generate docs/user-guides/US-0005.md with USER_GUIDE_MODE=1. The task should document the consolidated workflow, scope control, and Test Webhook integration.

findings.description: No automated UI tests planned for the monolithic HTML refactor
findings.impact: Manual regression testing required — increases verification time and risk of human error
findings.recommendation: Execute phase should include a manual regression checklist (already present in tasks.md). Consider adding automated UI tests in a future sprint if similar refactors are planned.

findings.description: Scope field is additive and optional — existing API callers unaffected
findings.impact: No breaking changes to REST contract
findings.recommendation: Verify during execute phase that existing callers (if any) continue to work without scope field. T-0039 correctly implements optional scope parameter.

verdict_summary.reason: All 8 acceptance criteria are covered by at least one task (AC-8 deferred to execute phase is acceptable). All architecture decisions (DEC-0015/0016/0017) are correctly referenced and implemented. Dependency graph is logical and complete. All 6 risks are addressed by tasks. Three low-severity findings documented (AC-8 documentation deferred, no automated UI tests, backward compatibility verification).
